headline: 92-94°F heat dome hits day-2 tomato transplants
weather_notes: Today 92°F and Tuesday 94°F with zero rain — day-2 tomato transplants face maximum stress with no natural moisture relief through Wednesday.
No precipitation through Tuesday (0%) — all beds depend entirely on irrigation; zone 1 and 2 were last watered May 9.
Peas (c2–c4) at peak flower — sustained 90°F+ temps trigger blossom drop and will reduce pod set for the June 3 harvest.
Wednesday brings a slight T-storm chance (14%) — if rain arrives, hold next irrigation and check soil moisture before watering again.

surfaced_tasks.title: Deep-water e1–e4 tomatoes before Monday heat
surfaced_tasks.reason: Overdue by one day — today IS the 92°F event and day-2 transplants need a deep soak now before Tuesday hits 94°F.

suggested_tasks.title: Mulch base of e1–e4 tomato transplants
suggested_tasks.description: A 2-3 inch mulch layer on day-2 transplants cuts soil temperature and halves moisture loss during back-to-back 90°F+ days.
